title: High remuneration is not prompted to argue a peer comparison
expected: For an industry self-petitioner whose strongest clear criterion is comp, the draft should argue High remuneration AGAINST field peers ('top-decile vs. ML scientists per <source>'), not assert it bare — a bare comp claim is a classic RFE magnet.
got: buildDraftPrompt enforces no-fabrication and no-case-law but contains no remuneration-specific instruction to frame a peer/percentile comparison; the High remuneration section will be as strong as the model's default, and the fabrication gate even flags a real comp figure that isn't byte-present in the persisted criterion evidence.
suggested_acceptance: The draft's High remuneration section frames the comp against field peers / a percentile band (when supplied), not a bare assertion; L2 fills a real comp band and reads the live output.
l2_priority: Put Fatima's real comp band (e.g. $320K base+equity, top-decile) through qualify->draft and assert the High remuneration section argues a peer/percentile comparison, not a bare 'commands a high salary'.

title: Industry specifics (patent topic, Kaggle Grandmaster) may flatten to generic across qualify->criteria->draft
expected: Patents argued as Original contribution and Kaggle Grandmaster recognized as a selective distinction, using her real specifics, not a professor's framing.
got: On the DB path the draft prompt is fed the persisted per-criterion evidence/rationale + vault exhibit facts, NOT the raw CV (draftOperation.ts:147-161). Whether 'patent on training-efficiency methods' and 'Kaggle Grandmaster' survive the qualify->criteria capture or flatten to 'an original contribution' is invisible to static analysis. This is the accepted PN-DRAFT-01 (BACKLOG G1.3) viewed through an industry-evidence lens, not the research lens L2 already proved.
suggested_acceptance: Live draft from an INDUSTRY profile names the patent's topic and the Kaggle Grandmaster rank as distinctions, weighting them over a modest citation count.
l2_priority: Run Fatima's industry record live; assert the draft surfaces the patent topic and Kaggle rank as concrete distinctions, not generic 'original contribution' filler.

title: Landing '/' certificate is a keyword mock with generic (non-named) evidence
expected: A verdict that names her real evidence (her patents, Kaggle rank, venues, comp).
got: The landing InstantVerdict posts to /api/qualify/preview which runs ONLY mockQualification (regex keyword match, Met/None only, templated generic evidence strings) — never the model. It shows 'Mentions a patent...', not 'two granted patents'. Honestly labelled an 'instant keyword read' with a soft gate to the real authenticated read, so it is by-design and disclosed, not deceptive; acceptance criterion 1 is met only on the authenticated /qualify path.
suggested_acceptance: The landing keyword read stays clearly labelled as a keyword scan (it is); the named-evidence read is the authenticated path. No change required — recorded so L2 doesn't mistake the generic landing copy for thin model grounding.
l2_priority: On authenticated /qualify with Fatima's paste, confirm the real Claude read names her patents/Kaggle/venues/comp (not the generic landing copy).

title: 'Certificate / Approved' framing over a regex read risks a horoscope read for a rigor-focused user
expected: A sober screening that a quantitative user reads as informational, not award theater.
got: The hero renders an engraved 'Certificate of Extraordinary Ability' + Seal around a deterministic keyword screening, and the marketing Hero card hardcodes an 'Approved / 92% likelihood' stamp (page.tsx:228). The honest scored data is underneath, but the ceremony framing can read as more than a regex. This is the OPEN BACKLOG item PN-QUAL-01 (G3.1) — referenced, not re-invented; subjective, L2 to judge.
suggested_acceptance: Per G3.1: L2 judges whether the certificate framing aids or hurts credibility for a rigor-focused user; soften toward 'informational screening' if it hurts.
l2_priority: Judge whether the certificate/Approved theater helps or hurts trust for a quantitative beneficiary (G3.1).

title: Refile to a wrong bucket silently inflates coverage
expected: Coverage reflects criteria genuinely proven; a manual refile to a bucket the doc doesn't support should not silently close that gap.
got: onRefile changes a doc's criterion with no sanity check; summarizeVault then counts the (possibly wrong) bucket as covering that criterion. Pre-existing OPEN BACKLOG item G2.2 / dc-evidence-02 — referenced, not a new finding.
suggested_acceptance: Distinguish 'documents present' from 'criterion proven', or flag a refiled doc whose facts don't match the new bucket (BACKLOG G2.2).
l2_priority: Confirm whether a manual mis-refile visibly misleads the coverage/gap read live.

title: Roadmap shows 'Evidence' as current even after a draft exists, when the vault is empty
expected: The stepper marks the stage matching what I actually did; after drafting, 'Drafted' (or later) should read as current/done.
got: caseRoadmap computes current = !hasEvidence ? EVIDENCE : !hasDraft ? DRAFTED : REVIEW, so a self-petitioner who drafts straight from the screening with no vault docs sees the stepper sitting on 'Evidence' while a full draft is on screen — mildly contradictory. The draft card itself is unambiguous, so it's a nit, not a block.
suggested_acceptance: Once a draft exists, the stepper does not regress to 'Evidence' as current (e.g. advance to Drafted, or mark Evidence done-or-skipped) so 'what now?' is never contradicted.
l2_priority: Draft a case with an empty vault; confirm whether the roadmap stepper visibly contradicts the on-screen draft.

title: Live fabrication / UPL / wrong-code / case-law adjudication on every paid generation, surfaced with exact reasons
expected: A guard against the pet peeves: fabricated citation count, invented patent number, asserted-bare comp, leaked wrong visa code.
got: runAdjudication scores every paid generation (fabrication scan of money/percent/year/big-int not traceable to input, UPL advice tripwire, wrong-code, case-law flag) and AdjudicationBadge shows the user the exact reasons, defaulting open when not 'ready'. Directly protects Fatima's pet peeves.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: do not weaken the fabrication/UPL gates or hide the reasons UI.
l2_priority: Confirm a deliberately fabricated number trips the no-fabrication warn live and is shown to the user.

title: Met/Strong-only threshold math; unscored criteria never render green
expected: Nothing I didn't supply shows as 'Met'; the eight criteria and the >=3 threshold are correct.
got: A single classifyStatus drives both the badge tone and the summary count, so 'None'/unknown can never paint green or inflate the qualifying count; the pack threshold is passed through everywhere. Directly satisfies acceptance criteria 2 and 5.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep the single-source classifier; never let the table tone and summary count diverge.
l2_priority: Spot-check that a 'None' criterion renders neutral and is excluded from the qualifying count live.

title: Section regenerate preserves other edits and has continuity context
expected: Regenerate one section without losing my other edits or contradicting the rest of the letter.
got: The regenerate path sends the client's CURRENT sections; the server merges into them (pickMergeBase) and passes the other sections as read-only LETTER_CONTEXT so the new section stays consistent (G1.1/dc-draft-02 fixed). Edits stay local until 'Save edits', clearly stated.
suggested_acceptance: Protect: keep the current-sections merge base and the continuity context on regenerate.
l2_priority: Confirm a regenerated section does not duplicate the intro and that other unsaved edits survive.
